metadata.title: Harden Version History Schema and Sync Semantics

story.iWant: the version-history schema and sync overwrite semantics hardened immediately after Story 2.3
story.soThat: later sync, status, and rollback work builds on the correct event-history contract instead of carrying avoidable data-loss and metadata bugs forward
story.tasks: - Add GcalUpdatedAt, RecurringEventId, and IsRecurringInstance to gcal_event_version - Update SyncManager snapshots to copy the new fields - Stop sync overwrite/delete paths from resetting AppCreated, AppPublished, and SourceSystem - Change the gcal_event_version FK away from cascade delete - Clean up EventDataJson planning drift - Leave rollback implementation itself for Epic 8, but document snapshot-before-rollback and optional full-payload archival as future work

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Each gcal_event_version snapshot row also stores GcalUpdatedAt, RecurringEventId, and IsRecurringInstance.
Sync overwrite and delete handling no longer forcibly resets AppCreated, AppPublished, or SourceSystem on the live gcal_event row.
The gcal_event_version foreign key no longer cascades deletes from gcal_event.
Planning and implementation docs stop describing EventDataJson as the active version-history contract.
Rollback implementation is still deferred, but the future requirements to snapshot before rollback and optionally archive full observed Google payloads are documented explicitly.

doc.title: Story 2.3
doc.snippet: Story 2.3 standardized the current sync-history contract around the existing fielded GcalEventVersion table and explicitly rejected EventDataJson for that implementation.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Epic 2 excludes save/restore rollback implementation, so Story 2.3A should harden the sync contract without partially implementing Epic 8.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: The epic breakdown still contains older EventDataJson language that should be aligned to the implemented snapshot-table design before more story generation propagates the drift.

doc.title: Story 1.3
doc.snippet: Story 1.3 still contains a historical cascade-delete example and older rollback wording that now needs to reflect the hardened event-history contract.

file.reason: Contains GcalUpdatedAt, RecurringEventId, IsRecurringInstance, and local ownership fields that define the live-row source values and preservation rules.
Needs the additional fields required for better rollback fidelity and recurring-event history.
Currently configures cascade delete from gcal_event to GcalEventVersion and must be changed.
Needs new column mappings for gcal_updated_at, recurring_event_id, and is_recurring_instance.
Owns snapshot creation and currently wipes AppCreated, AppPublished, and SourceSystem during overwrite/delete handling.
Already covers update/delete/new/unchanged history behavior and should be extended rather than creating a second test harness.

constraints.constraint: This story is inserted after Story 2.3 and should land before Stories 2.4 and 2.5 so later sync work inherits the hardened contract.
Keep gcal_event_version as a fielded snapshot table. Do not reintroduce EventDataJson, ChangeType, or a second history table here.
AppCreated, AppPublished, and SourceSystem are local metadata. GoogleWins applies to Google-facing event content, not to wiping those local ownership fields.
Changing FK delete behavior is a safety improvement; soft delete in gcal_event remains the standard operational path for Google deletions.
Do not implement SaveRestoreService or rollback UI here. Only document that rollback should snapshot current live state before restore.
If full historical Google-payload archival is added later, treat it as explicit local capture of every observed Google version, not as a hidden expansion of the operational history table.

tests.standards: xUnit + FluentAssertions with SQLite-backed integration coverage for persistence and relationship behavior.

ideas.test: Seed a recurring instance row with GcalUpdatedAt, RecurringEventId, and IsRecurringInstance set, run an update sync, and assert the inserted history row preserves those original values.
Seed a row with AppCreated = true, AppPublished = true, and SourceSystem = manual, run a Google overwrite sync, and assert those fields remain intact on the live row.
Seed a row with local ownership flags, process a deleted Google event, and assert soft delete occurs without wiping AppCreated, AppPublished, or SourceSystem.
Verify the EF model / SQLite FK definition does not cascade delete history rows when the parent is removed.
Optional documentation regression check or manual review step to ensure story-generation source docs no longer instruct developers to use EventDataJson.
